overview.title: The complete herbalist, or, the people their own physicians by the use of nature's remedies showing the great curative properties of all herbs, gums, balsams, barks, flowers and roots ; how they should be prepared, when and under what influences selected, at what times gathered, and for what diseases administered. Phelps Brown.

retrieval_text: Wild Indigo is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 41 works and 82 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Wild Indigo. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Bark, Leaf, Flower, Gum, Berry. Common preparation co-mentions include Poultice, Decoction, Tea, Tincture, Powder, Oil. Common condition co-mentions include Wounds or ulcers, Inflammation, Cold or catarrh, Dysentery, Diarrhoea or looseness, Fever. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Fatal-risk warning, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include Popular medicine, or, The American family physician : a work in which the principles and practice of medicine are familiarly explained : comprising a complete and comprehensive description of diseases, with full and definite directions for their treatment : the properties of many new and important medicines, with their doses and mode of administration, are accurately described : designed for general use and adapted to all climates; The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions; Gunn's New domestic physician, or, Home book of health : a complete guide for families, giving many valuable suggestions for avoiding disease and prolonging life, and pointing out in familiar language the causes, symptoms, treatment and cure of the diseases incident to men, women and children : with the simplest and best remedies, also, describing minutely the properties and uses of hundreds of well-known medicinal plants; Gunn's new family physician, or, Home book of health : forming a complete household guide, giving many valuable suggestions for avoiding disease and prolonging life, with plain directions in case of energency, and pointing out in familiar language the causes, symptoms, treatment and cure of diseases incident to men, women, and children with the simplest and best remedies : presenting a manual for nursing the sick, and describing minutely the properties and uses of hundreds of well-known medicinal plants : with supplementary treatises on anatomy, physiology, and hygiene, on domestic and sanitary economy, and on physical culture and development. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
